title: frangula caroliniana
intro: Frangula caroliniana, also known as Carolina buckthorn, is a type of shrub or small tree that is native to eastern North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its laxative properties, as it contains anthraquinone glycosides, which stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation. The medicinal properties of Frangula caroliniana have been shown to have various health benefits, including relieving symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, reducing inflammation, and supporting the immune system. Frangula caroliniana can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, with the dried root bark being the most commonly used part of the plant for herbal preparations.
